era: 2001-2007. Capitol Hill, Seattle. Post-Punk. E=5. Post-Punk returns (last seen degree 52, 63-degree gap). Capitol Hill streak closes (110-115, six consecutive, ALL-TIME RECORD). Matador Records first entry. Named after Kerouac / Smiths.

location.formative: Capitol Hill's early-2000s post-punk scene. The neighborhood's density of venues, musicians, and cross-pollination between bands. Derek Fudesco from Murder City Devils. The musician network that made the band possible.
location.career: Pretty Girls Make Graves -- post-punk band. 2001-2007. Lookout! Records then Matador Records. Three albums. Dissolved 2007. Capitol Hill.

associated_musicians.name: Haunted Horses
associated_musicians.note: Industrial/Noise (114, E=5) and Post-Punk (115, E=5). The streak's final pair. Noise vs. elegance. Both Capitol Hill.

theory_nodes.concept: The burning elegance (one hundred sixteenth acoustic element -- angular post-punk architecture performed with unsustainable intensity; the music is both elegant and burning; Zollo's voice transforms architecture into emotion; interlocking guitar lines create rhythmic geometric patterns; controlled combustion -- passion and precision simultaneously)
examples: Pretty Girls Make Graves (degree 115) -- post-punk. Angular guitars + commanding vocals + rhythmic urgency. E=5. Controlled combustion.
Contrast with the hypnotic engine (degree 110, Kinski) -- patient accumulation. The burning elegance is immediate urgency.
Contrast with the collapse frequency (degree 109, No-Anchor) -- structural collapse. The burning elegance maintains structure under pressure.
Contrast with the punk circuit (degree 108, Veronica Lipgloss) -- explosive. The burning elegance is architecturally complex.
theory_nodes.description: The burning elegance is the 116th acoustic element. Angular post-punk architecture burning with unsustainable intensity.

ancestors: The angular lattice (degree 52, prior post-punk) -- if established, the burning elegance adds combustion.
The hypnotic engine (degree 110, Kinski) -- patience. The burning elegance is urgency.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred sixteen acoustic elements across one hundred fifteen degrees. Track element 117 at degree 116.

theory_nodes.concept: The streak closer (one hundred sixteenth artist-city pattern -- long neighborhood streak closes with a band that embodies the neighborhood's cultural identity; post-punk is inherently communal; requires a scene, a neighborhood, specific clubs and nights; the closer is the neighborhood)
examples: Capitol Hill streak (110-115): Kinski, Helms Alee, 764-HERO, Minus the Bear, Haunted Horses, Pretty Girls Make Graves.
PGMG closes with post-punk -- a genre inherently communal, requiring a neighborhood to exist.
The closer embodies the neighborhood: formed from the musician network, played the venues, lived the apartments.
Six-degree streak is the all-time record for neighborhood concentration.
theory_nodes.description: The streak closer is the 116th artist-city pattern. Neighborhood streaks close with bands that embody the neighborhood.

ancestors: Capitol Hill streak (110-115). Six consecutive. All-time record.
genealogy.relationship: One hundred sixteen artist-city patterns across one hundred fifteen degrees. Track pattern 117 at degree 116.

theory_nodes.concept: Post-Punk returns at degree 115 -- 63-degree gap since degree 52; the longest gap for a recurring genre; mirrors the genre's own history (late-1970s origin, underground period, early-2000s revival); angular guitar work, rhythmic urgency, strong female vocals
examples: Post-Punk at degree 52 and degree 115. 63-degree gap.
Pretty Girls Make Graves as the 2000s post-punk revival in Seattle -- Interpol (NY), Yeah Yeah Yeahs (NY), PGMG (Seattle).
The gap mirrors the genre's own hibernation and revival cycle.
Zollo's vocals continue post-punk's tradition of strong female vocalists: Siouxsie Sioux, Poly Styrene, Ari Up.
theory_nodes.description: Post-Punk returns after a 63-degree gap. Genre recurrence. Early-2000s revival of late-1970s/early-1980s genre.

ancestors: Prior post-punk entry (degree 52). The genre's first engine appearance.
genealogy.relationship: Post-Punk at degrees 52 and 115. 63-degree gap. Track.

theory_nodes.concept: Matador Records enters the engine at degree 115 -- first Matador entry; one of America's most significant independent labels; Pavement, Cat Power, Interpol, Yo La Tengo; Seattle's reach beyond the PNW; Capitol Hill to national audience
examples: Pretty Girls Make Graves on Matador for The New Romance (2003) and Elan Vital (2006).
Matador's national roster: Pavement, Guided by Voices, Cat Power, Interpol, Yo La Tengo.
Seattle's Capitol Hill scene reaching national audience through a New York-based label.
Contrast with Sub Pop (Seattle-based, most recurring label). Matador represents the external validation.
theory_nodes.description: Matador Records first entry at degree 115. Seattle's reach beyond the PNW. Capitol Hill to national audience.

ancestors: Sub Pop (most recurring label in the engine). Matador is the external counterpart.
genealogy.relationship: First Matador entry at degree 115. Track.

cross_references.relationship: CONSECUTIVE CAPITOL HILL. Industrial/Noise (114, E=5) and Post-Punk (115, E=5). The streak's final pair. Noise vs. elegance. Both Capitol Hill.
POST-PUNK RETURNS. 63-degree gap. The genre resurfaces after the longest absence for a recurring genre. 2000s revival revisits late-1970s/early-1980s origin.
CAPITOL HILL INDIE LABEL ADJACENCY. Both Capitol Hill bands with national independent label connections. The Capitol Hill-to-national pipeline.
STREAK BOOKENDS. Kinski opens (110); PGMG closes (115). Krautrock to Post-Punk. Hypnosis to burning. The streak's full range.
